title: malus torminalis
intro: Malus torminalis, also known as the bitter cherry or wild cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the Rosaceae family. Traditionally, its bark and leaves have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Malus torminalis have been known to provide relief from diarrhea, fever, and other gastrointestinal problems, as well as to aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. Malus torminalis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which are commonly used in herbal preparations for its medicinal benefits.
